key_insight: Black phosphorus’s momentum-resolved quantum geometry can be linked to a measurable low-loss EELS quantum weight, providing an experimentally accessible bulk signature of Wannier quantum geometry.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical way to probe and compare anisotropic electronic structure in layered materials using low-loss EELS, even when band-resolved metrics are obscured by degeneracies.
method: Built a 32-band DFT–Wannier Hamiltonian for black phosphorus.
Computed quantum metric and quantum weight from analytic momentum derivatives, including occupied-manifold projectors.
Introduced a restricted quantum weight Kii(ωc) that excludes the zero-loss region and satisfies the restricted Souza–Wilkens–Martin sum rule.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports nearly isotropic in-plane quantum weight, Kzz/Kxx = 0.972 ± 0.005, and a small but resolved +0.46% per eV U drift under Hubbard–Hartree corrections.
watch_item: Single-band quantum metrics can become singular or misleading in the conventional cell due to folding and near-degeneracies, so masked or occupied-manifold analyses are required.
paper_title: Quantum weight and low-loss EELS signatures of Wannier quantum geometry in black phosphorus
